Interface HTTPServer

All Superinterfaces:
Runnable
All Known Implementing Classes:
MyHTTPServer
public interface HTTPServer extends Runnable
Contract for a minimal, servlet-based HTTP server.

This interface defines the registration and lifecycle API for an embeddable HTTP server. Implementations accept incoming TCP connections, parse requests, and dispatch them to registered Servlet handlers. The server extends Runnable so it can run on its own thread.

Supported HTTP methods for servlet registration are GET, POST, and DELETE (case-insensitive at registration time). URI routing uses longest prefix match: the registered URI prefix with the greatest length that matches the start of the request path is selected. See MyHTTPServer for the reference implementation.

Typical usage:


 HTTPServer server = new MyHTTPServer(8080, 5);
 server.addServlet("GET", "/app/", new HtmlLoader("html_files"));
 server.addServlet("POST", "/upload", new ConfLoader());
 server.start();
 // ... application runs ...
 server.close();
See Also:

    void

    Shuts down the server gracefully.

    Starts the server and begins accepting client connections.

    run

  • Method Details

    • addServlet

      void addServlet(String httpCommanmd, String uri, Servlet s)
      Registers a servlet to handle requests for the given HTTP method and URI prefix.

      If a servlet is already registered for the same method and URI, it is replaced.

      Parameters:
      httpCommanmd - the HTTP method name (e.g. "GET", "POST", "DELETE")
      uri - the URI path prefix to match (e.g. "/app/")
      s - the servlet instance that will handle matching requests

    • removeServlet

      void removeServlet(String httpCommanmd, String uri)
      Removes a previously registered servlet mapping.

      If no mapping exists for the given method and URI, this method has no effect.

      Parameters:
      httpCommanmd - the HTTP method name of the mapping to remove
      uri - the URI path prefix of the mapping to remove

    • start

      void start()
      Starts the server and begins accepting client connections.

      The threading model is implementation-defined. In MyHTTPServer, this starts the accept loop on a background thread.

    • close

      void close()
      Shuts down the server gracefully.

      Implementations should stop accepting new connections, terminate or drain worker threads, and invoke Servlet.close() on all registered servlets to release their resources.
